pages.text: good evening everyone welcome to our November 13 Town Council meeting quick this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with section 5 of the open public meetings Act chapter 231 public law 1975 the annual notice was emailed to the star-ledger and filed in the township clerk's office on October 25th 2017 and published in the West Orange Chronicle on November 2nd 2017 Councilwoman castle Ino present councilman Garino present councilman Kurkova access council president McCartney please let the record show that councilman cirillo will not be in attendance this evening good evening everyone first thing on our conference agenda is a mayor's Proclamation and my colleague for a pancreatic Awareness Month thought I'm not sure how many years we've been doing this but we welcome you and Jerry has a proclamation thank you thank you Kelso president my colleagues good to see you again thank you again to be you know Thank You council president my colleagues todd c

ohen and your family your brother again thank you for being here it's been many years that I've had the pleasure of making this proclamation for pancreatic cancer awareness you know this year alone in in coming up to 2020 you know 50,000 new cases will be diagnosed and then sadly say 44,000 will die and it takes people like yourself with such integrity and heart and compassion to try to someday maybe find a cure but whatever your you do you're always making it that there's a comfort level that people in patients with pancreatic cancer don't feel alone they don't feel abandoned and that's the most important thing and you're to be commended along with your entire family for what you do and what you speak for and that pancreatic patient know that you're there for them and they're not alone proclamations are important you know people think they're just words and everything else but I can honestly tell you that along with my council colleagues in the administration and the members of the entire West Orange Township community you know we sincerely backed this and we

fully support it and we're always here we personally may we have personal affections to pancreatic cancer we understand the impact and the loss but you never give up hope and I hope that you never give up hope and that the rest of the community particularly the West Orange Community never gives up hope so if if I may I'd like to read the mayor's Proclamation whereas in 2018 sorry that these are not reading glasses okay whereas in 2018 an estimated 55 thousand four hundred people will be diagnosed with pancreatic cancer in the United States and 44,000 330 will die from the disease whereas pancreatic cancer is one of the deadliest cancers is currently the third leading cause of cancer death in the United States and is projected to become the second leading cause around the world in 2020 where as pancreatic cancer is the only major cancer with a five-year relative survival rate in the single digits at just 9 percent whereas when symptoms of pancreatic cancer present themself it is generally in later stages and only 10 percent of pancreatic cancer patients 87 00:

are diagnosed when a disease is confined to the pancreas and 9 point 1 percent of the pancreatic cancer patients die within the first 5 years whereas approximately 1,300 deaths will occur in New Jersey in 2018 and whereas pancreatic cancer is a seventh most common cause of cancer related deaths across the world whereas there will be an estimated four hundred and eighteen thousand new pancreatic cancer cases diagnosed worldwide in 2020 whereas the good health and well-being of the residents of West Orange or enhanced has a direct result of increased awareness about the symptoms and risk of pancreatic cancer and research into early detection causes an effective treatments therefore be resolved that the town designates November 15 2018 as world pancreatic cancer day in the township of West Orange respectfully submitted Mayor Robert E Parisi mayor of the township of West Orange thank you thank you yeah my brother Lee works at lip at Western high school I work at Liberty so kids always can ask me you were the Kelly yes or not
